---
title: "About RECAST"
description: "What RECAST is, who built it, and how to read this site."
---

**RECAST** — *Replication and Extension with Causal AI Statistical Toolkit* — is an
autonomous, multi-agent pipeline that takes a published econometrics paper and its
replication data and produces: a faithful replication of the original result, a
causal-machine-learning extension (Double/Debiased ML and generic ML for
heterogeneity), a structured AI peer review of that extension, and an honest report.

The aim is not to "beat" original papers with machine learning. It is to ask a
narrower, more useful question: **once you relax the functional form of the controls
with cross-fitted ML — while keeping the paper's own identification — does the
headline result survive, strengthen, weaken, or fall apart?** And to answer it with
an audit trail and an adversarial review that flags its own limits.

## How to read this site

- [**How it works**](methodology.qmd) — the four-step pipeline and the two-referee
  review.
- [**Papers**](papers.qmd) — four published results put through the full pipeline,
  each with its gap table, figures, referee reports, and an honest verdict.
- [**Coverage**](methodology/coverage.qmd) — which identification strategies have
  actually been tested, and which have not.

Every figure and table on the paper pages is generated from committed artifacts
(result JSONs, referee reports, frozen estimates), so the numbers are traceable.

## Credits

Built by [Quentin Gallea, Ph.D.](https://thecausalmindset.com) with
[Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code), rendered with
[Quarto](https://quarto.org). The methodology builds on Baiardi & Naghi (2024),
Chernozhukov et al. (2018), Chernozhukov, Demirer, Duflo & Fernández-Val (2018),
and the refereeing principles of Berk, Harvey & Hirshleifer (2017).
